_description: Single source of truth for agent design, build, and customer reporting. Sections follow the customer meeting flow. Every dropdown has a mapped purpose.

business.useCase: One-line use case for headers, report titles, and exec summaries
business.problemStatement: 2-3 sentence problem statement — what pain this agent solves, for whom, and why now

agent.name: Professional agent name
agent.description: MCS agent description — what the agent does, for whom (this is what appears in Copilot Studio)
agent.persona: Tone and personality (e.g., professional, concise, uses bullet points)
agent.responseFormat: How the agent formats responses (e.g., bullet points, max 3 items, tables for data, confirm before actions)
agent.primaryUsers: Main user group who will interact most frequently
agent.secondaryUsers: Supporting user group with occasional use

capabilities._meeting: Section 3 — 'What should it do?' (each flagged MVP or Future)
capabilities.name: Capability name (e.g., Look up order status)
capabilities.reason: Why MVP or why deferred — shown next to Future items (e.g., pending security approval)
capabilities.description: Optional detail for the build — PE uses this for instruction writing
dataSources: Systems this capability reads from or writes to

integrations._meeting: Section 4 — 'What systems does it connect to?'
integrations.name: Tool or integration name (e.g., SAP, SharePoint/OneDrive)
integrations.purpose: What data/actions this provides to the agent
integrations.dataProvided: Specific data fields: order number, status, ETA, tracking number
integrations.authMethod: OAuth 2.0 | API Key | Service Principal | User delegated | None

knowledge._meeting: Section 5 — 'What does it need to know?'
knowledge.name: Source name or URL
knowledge.type: SharePoint | Uploaded files | Dataverse | Public websites | Graph connectors
knowledge.purpose: What questions this knowledge source answers

topics.name: Topic name (e.g., Order Lookup)
topics.schemaName: MCS schema name (e.g., cr_orderlookup) — generated during build
topics.description: What this conversation flow does (used by 'agent chooses' trigger matching)
topics.triggerType: agent-chooses | phrases | fallback | redirect | escalation | event | auto-start | inactivity
implements: Capability names this topic implements (links to capabilities[].name)

variables.name: Variable name (e.g., orderNumber)
variables.type: string | number | boolean | choice | date | entity
variables.prompt: Question to ask the user (e.g., What's your order number?)

connectedIntegrations: Integration names this topic calls (links to integrations[].name)

handle: Topics the agent should answer confidently

decline.redirect: Where to redirect (e.g., Transfer to billing team via ServiceNow)

refuse.topic: Hard stop — agent must never do this
refuse.reason: Why this is refused (e.g., GDPR compliance)

architecture._meeting: Section 8 — 'How is it built?' Walk through 6 factors with the customer.
architecture.reason: Why this architecture type was chosen (e.g., 3/6 factors checked — domainSeparation + reusability + teamOwnership)

factors._description: 6-factor scoring — check each with the customer. 0-2 checked = Single Agent, 3+ = Multi-Agent.

architecture.buildPathReason: Why this build path was selected — include why alternatives were ruled out (e.g., 'declarative-agent: requires external channels; first-party-only: no coverage for expense reconciliation')

frontierAgentMatch.agentName: First-party or frontier agent name (e.g., Microsoft 365 Copilot HR agent)
frontierAgentMatch.recommendation: Use as-is | Extend with custom capabilities | Build custom instead
frontierAgentMatch.licenseRequired: License or SKU required (e.g., Microsoft 365 Copilot)
matchedCapabilities: Capability names already covered by this agent

triggers.description: When and why this trigger fires

channels.name: Microsoft Teams | M365 Copilot | Web chat | Direct Line | Slack | Mobile app

children.name: Child agent name (for multi-agent only)
children.routingRule: When to route to this specialist

evalSets._description: 5 default sets (critical, functional, integration, conversational, regression) + custom sets
evalSets.name: critical | functional | integration | conversational | regression | <custom>

methods.type: General quality | Compare meaning | Keyword match | Text similarity | Exact match | Capability use

evalSets.runWhen: every-iteration | per-capability | after-tools | after-functional | final | custom

tests.question: Test question to ask the agent
tests.expected: What the response should contain or convey
tests.capability: Which capability this tests (links to capabilities[].name, optional)

mvpSummary._description: Computed summary of all phase-flagged items. Generated by /mcs-research, used by /mcs-build as a quick checklist.
now: MVP items to build this sprint (names from capabilities, integrations, topics)
blockers: What blocks future items (e.g., pending security approval)

openQuestions.question: Unresolved question that needs customer/stakeholder input
openQuestions.impact: What this blocks or affects if unanswered
openQuestions.section: Which brief section this affects (e.g., integrations, capabilities)
openQuestions.suggestedDefault: Our best guess if the customer doesn't answer

decisions._description: Architecture, integration, channel, and UX decisions requiring customer input or confirmed during build. Referenced by /mcs-report and tracked by /mcs-retro DECISION_OUTCOME.
decisions.title: Decision title (e.g., Deployment mode)
decisions.recommended: Recommended option with 1-sentence reason (e.g., 'Solution-level — multi-agent topology requires package integrity')
decisions.selected: Option selected by customer or confirmed during build (empty until resolved)
decisions.notes: Additional context, constraints, or outcome notes

instructions: Full agent instructions text (up to 8000 chars). Written by Prompt Engineer, reviewed by QA Challenger. This is what gets pasted into MCS.

buildStatus.lastBuild: ISO timestamp of last build attempt
buildStatus.accountId: session-config account id (e.g. admin-m365) — used for PAC CLI profile lookup
buildStatus.publishedAt: ISO timestamp of last successful publish
buildStatus.lastCompletedStep: Last step that succeeded (e.g. tools) — used for resume
buildStatus.lastError: Optional — what failed on last attempt, cleared on next successful step

deployStatus.targetAccountId: session-config account id for target environment
deployStatus.targetBotId: MCS bot ID in target environment (set after agent-mode deploy)
deployStatus.deployedAt: ISO timestamp of last successful deployment

notes._description: Flexible key-value store for anything that doesn't fit above. Keys are freeform.
